description: Direct question to audience about food ordering preferences or consumption habits, designed to spark comment engagement and reveal customer behavior patterns.
structural_recipe: Post an image of menu items or dishes. Ask a casual, relatable question in Arabic about customer preferences (e.g., 'What do you usually start with?' or 'Which item do you prefer?'). Use emoji for visual emphasis. Include relevant food/restaurant hashtags. Keep tone conversational and friendly.
why_it_works: Saudis engage with content that invites personal opinion sharing and social proof. Food choices are identity-affirming in Saudi culture. Questions make followers feel heard and create FOMO when others comment their preferences. Low-barrier interaction increases algorithm visibility.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure questions are appropriate to Islamic dietary guidelines and cultural norms
Avoid questions that could be seen as judgmental about food choices
Questions should focus on preference, not price sensitivity
cultural_constraints: Respect halal/Islamic food preparation standards in context
Avoid gendered assumptions in questions (mixed dining culture varies)
Use Modern Standard Arabic or Saudi dialect appropriately
